title: A pairwise dedup or uniqueness check run on a single item never finds a collision
rule: A near-duplicate, uniqueness, or collision check that only compares the inputs you hand it is a no-op when you pass a single new item: with nothing to pair it against it reports clean regardless, so a local pre-check disagrees with the set-wide run in CI. Always run such checks against the full existing set plus the candidate, not the candidate alone, so a green local result actually matches what CI will compute.
triggers: duplicate check passes locally but the same check fails in CI
running the uniqueness check on one new file reports clean
near-duplicate detector finds nothing when given a single item
local gate is green but CI blocks a near-duplicate
